title: cebatha carolina
intro: Cebatha carolina, also known as American wormseed or Chenopodium ambrosioides, is a plant species that belongs to the amaranth family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as worms, parasites, and digestive problems. The plant contains compounds like ascaridole, which has been shown to have anthelmintic properties, making it effective against intestinal worms and other parasites. Herbal preparations of Cebatha carolina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of parasitic infections.
